popularStory.headline: Welf of Bavaria (d 1101) Bio Sketch
popularStory.description:
BIO:  Duke of Bavaria, 1070.  A crusader, died in Cyprus on a crusade.

** from http://fmg.ac/Projects/MedLands/BAVARIA.htm#HeinrichXBavariadied1139B, as of 10/29/2014

zh.lifeSummary: 當Herzog Welf von Bayern I於1037年出生在義大利維圖維其亞艾斯特時，他的父親Alberto Azzo II d'Este28歲，母親Kunigunde von Altdorf17歲。他於1070年之後與Judith de Flandre結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1101年11月6日在塞浦路斯帕福斯享年64歲，埋葬在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Herzog Welf von Bayern I于1037年出生在意大利維圖維其亞艾斯特时，他的父亲Alberto Azzo II d'Este28岁，母亲Kunigunde von Altdorf17岁。他于1070年之后与Judith de Flandre结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1101年11月6日在塞浦路斯帕福斯享年64岁，埋葬在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇。

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Bayern
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of German (and possibly also Dutch)   Bayer   or   Beyers  , or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname. Compare   Byers  .

variants.name: Bayer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Helmut, Kurt, Otto, Klaus, Manfred, Bernd, Bernhard, Erwin, Franz, Hans, Johann, Juergen.

German, Scandinavian, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Bavaria (German Bayern). This region of southern Germany derives its name from that of the Celtic tribe of the Boii who once inhabited this area. They were displaced in the 6th century AD by an ancient Germanic people, the Boioarii or Baiuarii, whose name is derived from that of their Celtic predecessors. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Czechia, Slovakia, and Poland. Compare Beyer and Payer .

English (Lincolnshire): occupational name for a maker of baize cloth, from an agent derivative of Old French baies, Middle English bayes (from the adjective bai ‘reddish-brown, bay’), probably so called because of its original color. This material was said to have been introduced into Britain by immigrants from France and the Netherlands in the 16th century, but the word certainly appears much earlier in English. The surname may also be topographic denoting a ‘dweller by the bend’, from an agent derivative of Old English bēag ‘bend’.

nameMeanings.name: Welf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the name of the animal (Old English wolf), in part a transferred use of the surname which originated as a nickname from this word, or an adoption of a German short form of any of the various compound names (such as Wolfgang and Wolfram ; ) with this first element.

en.lifeSummary: When Herzog Welf von Bayern I was born in 1037, in Este, Venice, Veneto, Italy, his father, Alberto Azzo II d'Este, was 28 and his mother, Kunigunde von Altdorf, was 17. He married Judith de Flandre after 1070. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 6 November 1101, in Paphos, Cyprus, at the age of 64, and was buried in Holy Roman Empire.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Herzog Welf von Bayern I est né en 1037, à Este, Venezia, Veneto, Italie, son père, Alberto Azzo II d'Este, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Kunigunde von Altdorf, avait 17 ans. Il a épousé Judith de Flandre après 1070. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 6 novembre 1101, à Paphos, Chypre, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterré à Saint-Empire romain.

de.lifeSummary: Als Herzog Welf von Bayern I 1037 in Este, Venezia, Veneto, Italien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Alberto Azzo II d'Este 28 und seine Mutter Kunigunde von Altdorf 17. Er hat nach 1070 Judith de Flandre geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 6. November 1101 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Paphos, Zypern und wurde in Heiliges Römisches Reich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Herzog Welf von Bayern I è nato il nel 1037 nel Este, Venezia, Veneto, Italia, il suo padre, Alberto Azzo II d'Este, aveva 28 anni e il suo madre, Kunigunde von Altdorf, aveva 17 anni. Lui sposato Judith de Flandre dopo il 1070. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 6 novembre 1101, nel Paphos, Cipro, all'età di 64 anni, e fu sepolto a Heiliges Römisches Reich.

ja.lifeSummary: Herzog Welf von Bayern Iが1037年にイタリアベンエトーベンエズアエストで生まれた時，父Alberto Azzo II d'Esteは28歳，母Kunigunde von Altdorfは17歳であった。1070年以後にJudith de Flandreと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1101年11月6日にキプロスプアフォスで64歳で死去し，ヘイリジェズ ルoミシェス レイチにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Herzog Welf von Bayern I(이)가 1037년 이탈리아 븐토 븐지아 에스트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Alberto Azzo II d'Este(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Kunigunde von Altdorf(은)는 17세였다. 그는 1070년 이후 Judith de Flandre(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1101년 11월 6일 키프로스 파포스에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 헤이리그스 o미슈스 라이치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Herzog Welf von Bayern I nasceu em 1037, em Este, Venezia, Veneto, Itália, seu pai, Alberto Azzo II d'Este, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Kunigunde von Altdorf, tinha 17 anos. Ele casou-se com Judith de Flandre após 1070. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 6 de novembro de 1101, em Paphos, Chipre, com 64 anos, e foi sepultado em Heiliges Römisches Reich.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Herzog Welf von Bayern I родился в 1037 г. в местности эст, винезя, винето, Италия, его отцу по имени Alberto Azzo II d'Este было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Kunigunde von Altdorf, 17. Он женился на женщине по имени Judith de Flandre после 1070 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 6 ноября 1101 г. в местности пафос, Кипр в возрасте 64 лет и был похоронен в местности хилигесь рoмищесь раич.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Herzog Welf von Bayern I nació en 1037, en Este, Venezia, Veneto, Italia, su papá, Alberto Azzo II d'Este, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Kunigunde von Altdorf, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Judith de Flandre después de 1070. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 6 de noviembre de 1101, en Paphos, Chipre, a la edad de 64 años, y fue sepultado en Heiliges Römisches Reich.
